title: Ensure that each log line is newline-terminated
body:
When writing logs with timestamps to the terminal, ensure that each line is newline-terminated, so we don't end up with an unreadable mess with timestamps interspersed with the actual content being displayed.

An partial fix of the (easy) part of the issue from #2500

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

The real fix here is probably to wait until we find a newline before printing, but that will probably require a complete rewrite of the output logic for --follow, so I'm going to avoid that part today

I found a more complete solution that emulates the expected output from Docker a lot more closely (though it's not without its quirks - Docker prints only when a complete line is entered, we print any time a character is entered, but only print a timestamp at the start of the line). It's a very close approximation, aside from our timestamps not matching (Docker uses time when the enter key was pressed, we use time when the first character of the line was typed).
@mheon The change you have made so far, code looks good.  But are you done?

@rhatdan I think this is good enough. The timestamps issue is annoying, but fixing it would require a wholesale rewrite of most of this code; we can do almost everything Docker can with this five or so line change. This should be ready for merge.
